skill.name: security-audit
skill.description: Guided security review skill for code, architecture, infrastructure, supply chain, smart contract, and ZK circuit assessments.
skill.summary: Run structured security reviews using established frameworks and domain checklists.
skill.author: ClementWalter

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk terms, but review showed they appear in security checklists, markdown examples, and audit guidance rather than executable code. No evidence found for malicious execution, data exfiltration, prompt injection, or hidden override instructions. The skill is publishable with low residual risk from security-sensitive terminology and review workflows.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Security Checklist Keywords
low_findings.description: The scanner matched terms such as deprecated algorithms, eval(), overflow, and transcript binding inside defensive checklist items. These lines instruct reviewers to find or prevent vulnerabilities and do not execute code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is markdown checklist text for defensive review. I found no executable script, dynamic evaluation path, or user-controlled command execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance and Attack Vocabulary
low_findings.description: The scanner matched security terms such as rate limit, flash loan, source integrity, and incident response. The context is educational audit criteria, not reconnaissance logic or command instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched phrases are part of security assessment vocabulary. No network scanner, target enumeration command, or external endpoint appears in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Formatting Matched as Execution
low_findings.description: Backticks and fenced text blocks were matched as shell or command indicators. The affected lines are documentation examples or checklist prose, not runnable code shipped by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line context shows markdown fences and inline code formatting. There is no script file, interpreter directive, or automation that would run these snippets.

content.value_statement: Security reviews can miss important risks when they lack structure. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through framework-based audits with focused checklists.
actual_capabilities: Guides threat modeling with STRIDE categories and trust boundary questions.
Reviews identity, secrets, cryptography, input handling, infrastructure, and logging controls.
Applies checklist coverage for web applications, supply chain, smart contracts, and ZK circuits.
Structures findings with severity, category, location, impact, recommendations, and references.
Maps security work to frameworks such as OWASP ASVS, NIST SSDF, CIS Controls, SLSA, and OpenSSF.
limitations: It provides review guidance and does not run scanners or exploit tests by itself.
Findings depend on the files, architecture notes, and context supplied by the user.
It cannot certify compliance or replace a formal third-party security audit.
Blockchain and ZK reviews still require domain expert validation for high-value systems.

use_cases.title: Review a pull request
use_cases.description: Check authentication, authorization, input handling, secrets, and logging changes before merge.

use_cases.title: Assess a build pipeline
use_cases.description: Evaluate dependency controls, branch protection, artifact signing, provenance, and CI secret exposure.

use_cases.title: Prepare a protocol audit
use_cases.description: Organize smart contract or ZK circuit risks before a deeper specialist review.

prompt_templates.title: Basic security review
prompt_templates.scenario: First pass over a small code change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this change for security risks. Focus on authentication, authorization, input validation, secrets, and logging. Return findings with severity and fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Threat model a feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Threat model this feature using STRIDE. Identify trust boundaries, assets, abuse cases, missing controls, and the highest-priority design changes.

prompt_templates.title: Audit supply chain controls
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this repository and pipeline against SLSA and OpenSSF practices. Cover dependencies, branch protection, secrets, artifacts, provenance, and release controls.

prompt_templates.title: Deep domain audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of cryptography, smart contracts, or ZK circuits.
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a domain-specific security audit of this design and code. Use the relevant checklist, explain exploit paths, rank findings, and propose concrete remediations.

output_examples.input: Review a new password reset flow.
output: High: Reset tokens are not single use, so replay can take over an account.
Medium: Rate limits are missing on token requests, which enables mailbox flooding.
Low: Audit logs do not record reset completion events.

output_examples.input: Assess a release pipeline before launch.
output: Critical dependency updates have no service-level target.
Release artifacts are checksummed but not signed.
Protected branch rules and required reviews reduce direct tampering risk.

output_examples.input: Threat model a smart contract upgrade mechanism.
output: The admin key is a single point of failure.
A timelock would give users time to react to malicious upgrades.
Monitoring should alert on queued and executed upgrade transactions.

best_practices: Provide architecture diagrams, changed files, dependency manifests, and deployment context before asking for review.
Ask for severity, exploit path, affected asset, and concrete remediation for every finding.
Validate high-impact findings with tests, logs, or a human security reviewer before release.
anti_patterns: Do not treat checklist completion as proof that a system is secure.
Do not ask for offensive exploitation steps against systems you do not own.
Do not ignore low-severity findings that repeat across many services.

faq.question: Does this skill run security scanners?
faq.answer: No. It guides manual review and can interpret scanner results that you provide.

faq.question: Can it review pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed to review code changes for security impact and missing controls.

faq.question: Which frameworks does it reference?
faq.answer: It references NIST CSF, CIS Controls, NIST SSDF, OWASP ASVS, MITRE ATT&CK, SLSA, and OpenSSF.

faq.question: Can it audit smart contracts?
faq.answer: It includes smart contract review guidance, but high-value contracts still need specialist review.

faq.question: Can it verify compliance?
faq.answer: No. It can organize evidence and gaps, but it does not provide legal or compliance certification.

faq.question: What should I provide for best results?
faq.answer: Provide code, architecture notes, threat assumptions, deployment details, dependencies, and existing scanner output.
